qa.overall_assessment: BA's analysis is structurally sound. All three factual claims verified against the actual script. Line numbers, function names, and default values are accurate. Scope is correctly bounded to a single file. One minor line-range discrepancy (restore_online_sessions scope) is non-blocking. One spec_text_vs_execution_drift observation for AC5/AC6 wording vs verifiable recipe is raised as a blocking objection per dimension 5.

ba_validation_objections.description: AC5 states 'THEN the unit is created with MemoryMax=4G, MemoryHigh=3G, MemorySwapMax=2G (not infinity) AND the log line at ~line 1455 confirms these values'. The log line reference (~line 1455) is not verifiable from the current script: the systemd-run invocation is at line 1424, and the only log line in daemon_spawn_session() that mentions memory values is implicit in the systemd-run flags — there is no explicit 'log ...' call that dumps MemoryMax/MemoryHigh/MemorySwapMax values after spawning. The AC's 'log line at ~line 1455' would need to be a NEW log statement (not an existing one). The AC criterion itself is correct but the literal text points at a log that does not yet exist. Dev verification of AC5 can only be done by reading the modified lines 1418-1420 (which IS verifiable) — the log-line verification recipe points at a non-existent artifact.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA should update AC5's 'AND the log line at ~line 1455 confirms these values' clause to: 'AND lines 1418-1420 of the modified script read recovery_memory_max="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_MAX:-4G}", recovery_memory_high="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_HIGH:-3G}", recovery_memory_swap_max="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_SWAP_MAX:-2G}".' This is the actual verifiable artifact. If BA intends for Dev to ADD a new log line (S1 says 'Add a log message'), the AC should state that explicitly.
ba_validation_objections._dimension_note: This is spec_text_vs_execution_drift: the AC literal text references a log line that does not currently exist in the script, making the verification recipe partially unexecutable as written. The substantive intent (verify finite defaults) is verifiable via code read; the specific recipe drifts from reality.

findings.claim: record_stable_loss_tombstones() is called only at line 792 inside watch_sessions(), guarded by loss_stable_count >= loss_required where loss_required=30 (line 749)
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. grep shows record_stable_loss_tombstones at line 359 (definition) and line 792 (sole call site). Direct read of line 749 confirms loss_required=30. Direct read of lines 786-800 confirms the >= 30 guard before the call.

findings.claim: loss_observe_mode transitions to 1 at line 833 with no tombstone write in that branch
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. grep for 'loss_observe_mode=1' returns line 833 exactly, inside 'elif [ "$nr" -gt 0 ]' branch. Lines 832-835 read directly: no tombstone write or call to record_stable_loss_tombstones in that branch.

findings.claim: compute_and_record_diff() computes removed set at line 609 but only returns na:nr count string
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. Direct read of lines 581-626: removed is computed at line 609 (comm -13 command), used for record_event calls at lines 616-620, and only na:nr is echoed at line 625. The removed variable is local and not returned.

findings.claim: daemon_spawn_session() returns 0 at line 1478 after tracking confirmed; no write_home_snapshot() call in restore_online_sessions() caller at lines 1760-1762
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. Direct read of line 1478: 'return 0' immediately after wait_for_daemon_tracking succeeds. Direct read of lines 1759-1765: after daemon_spawn_session returns success, only total_restored and globally_spawned[$uuid]=1 are set — no write_home_snapshot call. grep for write_home_snapshot confirms only two call sites: definition at line 340 and call at line 700 (in save path), never in restore_online_sessions.

CONFIRMED. Direct read of lines 1418-1420 shows exactly: 'local recovery_memory_max="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_MAX:-infinity}"', 'local recovery_memory_high="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_HIGH:-infinity}"', 'local recovery_memory_swap_max="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_SWAP_MAX:-infinity}"'. Comment at lines 1414-1417 matches BA's description exactly.

findings.claim: Server RAM: MemTotal 32088160 kB (~30.6 GiB), SwapTotal 41943032 kB (~40 GiB)
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. Direct grep of /proc/meminfo: MemTotal: 32088160 kB (line 1), SwapTotal: 41943032 kB (line 15). BA's values match exactly.
CONFIRMED. Direct read of the file shows the last line (2441) is the closing 'esac' with content ';;' and line 2441 is the final line. The script ends at line 2441.
CONFIRMED. Function definition starts at line 359, closing brace at line 386 (direct read confirms).

findings.claim: write_home_snapshot() at lines 340-353 uses atomic tmp+mv pattern
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. Direct read of lines 340-353: writes to '$home/$SESSION_FILE.tmp' then 'mv $home/$SESSION_FILE.tmp $home/$SESSION_FILE'. Atomic pattern is present.

findings.description: User requirement document contains 8 explicitly listed '烂账' (debt items) to fix in sequence. BA has selected three of these: tombstone timing (gap 1 maps to item 4: 'give manual kill/archive tombstones'), snapshot persistence (gap 2 maps to item 3: 'fix restore/adopt: all recovered sessions must enter daemon tracking'), and MemoryMax defaults (gap 3 maps to item 7: 'add per-session MemoryMax'). BA has correctly classified items 1, 2, 5, 6, 8 from the user's ordered list as Won't Have (W1-W4) or out of scope for this cycle. The user's original text says 'first fix the debts one by one', and BA has scoped this cycle to three specific, bounded, code-level fixes in a single file.
findings.note: The user requirement also asks 'tell me if the 6-hour job scan has fully recovered, but low overhead' — this is an informational query, not a code fix. BA correctly omits it from the fix scope. No scope creep detected.

findings.description: Universal scope check: user says 'all recovered sessions must enter daemon tracking' and 'per-session MemoryMax'. BA's fix targets the restore path in restore_online_sessions() (GAP 2) and daemon_spawn_session() memory defaults (GAP 3). This covers the recovery code path. The user's requirement for 'HOME/CODEX_HOME fix' (item 6 in their list) is NOT addressed by BA. BA does not list it as Won't Have or explicitly exclude it — it simply does not appear in scope.
findings.note: The HOME/CODEX_HOME env var issue from the user's list (item 6: 'HOME=/root, CODEX_HOME unset...high risk point') is absent from BA's scope without explicit Won't Have classification. This is an omission from BA's MoSCoW table. However, since the user's primary statement was to fix things 'one by one' (sequential, not simultaneous), and BA has selected three specific items, the omission is arguably cycle-scoping rather than a scope mismatch. Flagging as minor, not blocking.

findings.claim: BA verified memory defaults from actual code at lines 1418-1420
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. BA states 'evidence_type: inferred' and 'verified_via: Read' for this component. Direct read in this QA session confirms lines 1418-1420 have exactly the values BA claims. The 'inferred' label is appropriate — BA read the code but cannot run it live due to the bash safety hook.

CONFIRMED. BA's context JSON field 'environment.MemTotal_kB: 32088160' and 'SwapTotal_kB: 41943032' match /proc/meminfo exactly. The observation entry in root_cause_analysis.observations explicitly cites '/proc/meminfo read in this session'.

findings.claim: BA acknowledged runtime behavior cannot be directly observed due to bash safety hook
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. context JSON 'diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — cannot run the script live due to bash safety hook; runtime behavior not directly observed)'. This is honest and appropriate — the hook permanently blocks execution of happy-session-recovery.sh. BA's investigation is as complete as the environment allows.

findings.claim: BA verified write_home_snapshot has no call site in restore_online_sessions()
findings.verification: CONFIRMED. BA states 'grep for write_home_snapshot showed no call in restore_online_sessions()'. QA grep confirms: write_home_snapshot appears at lines 340 (definition) and 700 (save path call) only — never inside restore_online_sessions() which spans lines 1496-1775.

findings.claim: /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh is the correct and complete set of affected files
findings.verification: CONFIRMED for the three specific gaps in scope. All three gaps are self-contained in this file: tombstone logic is in watch_sessions() and record_stable_loss_tombstones() (both in this file), snapshot persistence gap is in restore_online_sessions() (in this file), and memory defaults are in daemon_spawn_session() (in this file). No import from or to other files for these three functions.

findings.claim: The daemon source at /root/happy/packages/happy-cli/src/daemon/run.ts is NOT in scope
findings.verification: ASSESSED. BA correctly excludes run.ts. The three gaps are entirely in the recovery script's Bash logic. The daemon's run.ts is relevant to understanding why tracking works (it receives /session-started webhooks), but the fix does not require changing it. BA's rationale in Won't Have W3 and W4 supports this exclusion.

MINOR DISCREPANCY. The function definition restore_online_sessions() starts at line 1496 (confirmed by grep), not 1486. Line 1486 is a comment header block. BA's component_chain entry shows 'line_range: 1486-1775' — the range encompasses the comment preamble starting at 1486, which is technically inclusive of the function's documentation. The closing brace is at line 1775. The discrepancy is 10 lines of comment header, not a factual error about what code is affected.
CONFIRMED. grep shows daemon_spawn_session() defined at line 1323. Direct read of lines 1323 and 1484 confirms: line 1323 is the function definition, line 1484 is the closing brace ('}').

findings.description: AC5 states 'AND the log line at ~line 1455 confirms these values'. The actual script has no log line at line 1455 (or nearby) that dumps MemoryMax/MemoryHigh/MemorySwapMax values. Line 1455 is within the systemd-run invocation block (which starts at line 1424). The only way to verify the memory values from a log-based recipe would be to add a new log statement — which is a Should Have (S1) in BA's MoSCoW, not a Must Have. The verifiable recipe for AC5 is: grep lines 1418-1420 of the modified script and confirm the defaults are '4G', '3G', '2G'. The AC literal text specifies a non-existent log line, creating a drift between the spec text and the actually-runnable verification approach.
findings.proposed_fix: Update AC5 clause from 'AND the log line at ~line 1455 confirms these values' to 'AND lines 1418-1420 of the modified /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh read: recovery_memory_max="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_MAX:-4G}", recovery_memory_high="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_HIGH:-3G}", recovery_memory_swap_max="${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_SWAP_MAX:-2G}"'. If BA intends Dev to add an explicit log line (per S1), add a separate clause specifying the log format and location.

findings.description: AC1 GIVEN/WHEN/THEN is logically precise and verifiable via code read. The 'THEN a tombstone entry is written to session_tombstones.jsonl' clause is verifiable by reading the code change (new tombstone write before sleep in loss_observe_mode=1 branch). No drift detected.
AC2 is verifiable: it tests is_tombstoned() behavior, which is a code-read verification. No drift.

AC3 states 'write_home_snapshot() (or equivalent) is called for the session's home'. The '(or equivalent)' hedge is appropriate given the implementation hint gives Dev flexibility. The atomic tmp+mv requirement is verifiable from the existing write_home_snapshot() implementation (confirmed at lines 340-353). No drift.

AC6 states the env var override is respected. This is verifiable via code read of lines 1418-1420: the '${HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_MAX:-4G}' bash default syntax preserves operator override by design. No drift.

minor_items: SA-2: HOME/CODEX_HOME env fix from user's list not classified as Won't Have in BA's MoSCoW (omission, not contradiction)
AFA-3: restore_online_sessions() line range stated as 1486-1775 vs actual function start at 1496 (10-line comment preamble included in range)
spec_drift_items: STD-1 (AC5): 'log line at ~line 1455' does not exist in current script; verifiable recipe is a code read of lines 1418-1420, not a log grep. BA should update AC5 literal text.
